symptoms.symptom_name: New cardiac symptoms two weeks after a viral illness (viral prodrome)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A recent viral illness preceding cardiac symptoms is characteristic of viral myocarditis.
Tachycardia out of proportion to fever suggests myocardial strain in myocarditis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive dyspnoea and severely reduced exercise tolerance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Breathlessness at low workload indicates early left-ventricular dysfunction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Variable, slightly positional chest ache (worse lying flat)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Positional chest pain suggests accompanying pericardial involvement (myopericarditis).

symptoms.symptom_name: No crushing/jaw pain or diaphoresis; no DVT risk/long-haul travel

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of crushing pain/diaphoresis and DVT risk lowers the likelihood of ACS/PE (still excluded in hospital).

symptoms.symptom_name: S3 gallop, borderline raised JVP, mild ankle oedema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An S3 with raised JVP indicates ventricular dysfunction/volume overload, distinguishing myocarditis from isolated pericarditis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated troponin and late gadolinium enhancement on cardiac MRI

investigation_details.investigation_name: Troponin, ECG, echocardiography and cardiac MRI
investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated troponin with ECG changes; echocardiographic LV dysfunction; cardiac MRI showing late gadolinium enhancement consistent with myocarditis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Myocardial injury/inflammation is not confirmed and monitoring/treatment cannot be guided.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Elevated troponin with late gadolinium enhancement on cardiac MRI confirms myocarditis.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute myocarditis with early LV dysfunction
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A viral prodrome followed by disproportionate resting tachycardia and progressive dyspnoea, with (sealed) an S3 and raised JVP, indicates myocarditis with early heart-failure signs requiring urgent hospital monitoring, strict rest and avoidance of NSAIDs.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Troponin and 12-lead ECG with continuous monitoring
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Detects myocardial injury and arrhythmia; monitoring is essential given sudden-deterioration risk.
Assesses LV function and excludes a pericardial effusion/tamponade.
Confirms myocardial inflammation (late gadolinium enhancement) - the diagnostic imaging for myocarditis.
NSAIDs are relatively contraindicated in myocarditis and home management forgoes essential monitoring.
